title: Dashboard Load Map
text:
Category: Mega-Polis → Visualisation → Visualisation Data Tools
Node ID: SvMegapolisDashboardLoadMap
Tooltip: Dashboard Load Map
Dependencies: (outputs a dashboard snippet; expects a compatible mapping library in the runtime where the snippet is executed)



Generates a Python code snippet (string) that creates/initialises a dashboard map object and assigns it to a variable name.
This node is designed to work as the starting point of a map dashboard pipeline, where subsequent nodes (e.g., Dashboard GeoJSON To Map) add layers to the created map.
It does not render a map inside Blender/Sverchok. The output must be executed in your dashboard environment (e.g., Streamlit app with a mapping library).











Socket
Type
Description




Map Name
SvStringsSocket
Variable name to assign the created map object to (e.g., m, map1). Must be linked.















Name
Type
Default
Description




Provider
Enum
OpenStreetMap
Basemap provider name (used in the generated snippet).


zoom
Int
12
Initial zoom level.


latitude
Float
0.0
Initial map center latitude.


longitude
Float
0.0
Initial map center longitude.














Socket
Type
Description




Dashboard Map
SvStringsSocket
Python code snippet that creates the map object and stores it in the given variable name.









Connect:

Map Name → "m"

Set:

Provider → OpenStreetMap
latitude → -37.8136
longitude → 144.9631
zoom → 12

Output snippet (conceptually) will create a map object centered at Melbourne, stored in m.

Then chain:

Dashboard GeoJSON To Map (Map Name = "m") to add layers.






This node outputs code only. You must execute it in your dashboard runtime.
The exact mapping backend depends on how your dashboard runner interprets the snippet (e.g., Folium, leafmap, ipyleaflet). Ensure your runtime provides the map constructor used in the generated code.
Use the same Map Name consistently across the map pipeline.

title: Dashboard Server
text:
Category: Mega-Polis → Visualisation → Visualisation Supporting Tools
Node ID: SvMegapolisDashboardServer
Tooltip: Dashboard Server
Dependencies: os, multiprocessing, streamlit



Launches a Streamlit dashboard server by running a generated dashboard script in a separate process.
This node is intended to be used after Dashboard Creation, once you have a .py dashboard script ready to run.
When enabled, it spawns a background process that executes something equivalent to:
streamlit run <dashboard_script.py>
It outputs a simple status message indicating whether the server is waiting or running.











Socket
Type
Description




Dashboard Path
SvStringsSocket
Full path to the dashboard script file (.py). Must be linked.















Name
Type
Default
Description




run
Bool
False
When enabled, launches the Streamlit server process.


port
Int
8501
Port used to serve the Streamlit app (if supported in implementation).














Socket
Type
Description




Output Message
SvStringsSocket
Status string indicating whether the dashboard is running.









Use Dashboard Creation to generate:

/tmp/megapolis_dashboard/urban_analysis.py

Connect that path to Dashboard Path.
Enable run.
Open the Streamlit server URL in your browser:

http://localhost:8501

title: Dashboard Creation
text:
Category: Mega-Polis → Visualisation → Visualisation Supporting Tools
Node ID: SvMegapolisDashboardCreation
Tooltip: Dashboard Creation
Dependencies: os (file writing). The generated script expects streamlit and any referenced libraries (Plotly, Bokeh, PyVista, etc.) in the runtime environment.



Creates a Streamlit dashboard script file by assembling previously generated dashboard code snippets into a single runnable .py file.
The node:

Receives a Folder path and a Dashboard Name
Receives one or more Dashboard Components (code snippets generated by other dashboard nodes)
Writes a Python file (e.g., <dashboard_name>.py) inside the given folder
Outputs the path to the created dashboard file

This node acts as the final assembler of the Mega-Polis dashboard pipeline.
It does not execute the dashboard — it only generates the script file.











Socket
Type
Description




Folder
SvStringsSocket
Path to the folder where the dashboard script will be created. Must be linked.


Dashboard Name
SvStringsSocket
Name of the dashboard file (without .py). Must be linked.


Dashboard Components
SvStringsSocket
List of Python code snippets generated by other dashboard nodes. Must be linked.



All inputs must be connected for the node to execute.






Name
Type
Default
Description




run
Bool
False
When enabled, creates the dashboard file.














Socket
Type
Description




Dashboard Path
SvStringsSocket
Full path to the generated Streamlit dashboard script.









Build dashboard components:

Dashboard Markdown
Dashboard Plotly Scatter
Dashboard DataFrame

Combine all snippet outputs into Dashboard Components.
Provide:

Folder → "/tmp/megapolis_dashboard"
Dashboard Name → "urban_analysis"

Enable run.
The node creates:

/tmp/megapolis_dashboard/urban_analysis.py
